info.topictitle: B4.1.3 Factors affecting manufacturing technique choice

markdown:
### Factors affecting manufacturing technique choice


1. When designing or making a product, **choosing the right manufacturing technique **is critical. 
2. Designers and manufacturers must weigh **technical, economic, and environmental** factors to select the most appropriate method.

| Factor | Explanation | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Product Type | Is the product simple, complex, functional, aesthetic, or a mix? This affects required precision and finish. | A watch face needs intricate detailing → CNC machining |
| Material Type | Some processes suit metals, others plastics or composites. Material properties (e.g. melting point, brittleness) influence technique. | Thermoplastics can be injection moulded, but ceramics require casting |
| Scale of Production | One-off, batch, or mass production influences cost-efficiency and tool investment. | Mass production → injection moulding; One-off → 3D printing |
| Production System | The wider system (e.g. assembly line, automation, CIM) must support the selected technique. | CIM system integrates robotic arms and laser cutters for car parts |
| Cost Constraints | Budget affects tooling, labour, and materials. High-volume automation may have higher upfront costs but lower per-unit costs. | Laser cutting may be cheaper than machining for sheet parts |
| Environmental Considerations | Energy use, material waste, and recyclability of the method all matter. | 3D printing reduces waste compared to subtractive techniques |

<callout type="note">#### JIT vs JIC Inventory Strategies


* **Just-In-Time (JIT)**:
  * Raw materials and components are delivered only when needed.
  * Reduces inventory cost but needs reliable suppliers.
  * Used in **lean, efficient systems** like Toyota_._
* **Just-In-Case (JIC)**:
  * Extra stock is held to avoid delays.
  * Higher storage cost, but safer when supply is uncertain.
  * Used in **unpredictable supply chains** (e.g. pandemic-era manufacturing)_._

![Toyota's JIT production model](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/2ab4931e6c246ff8f87acea73c642f4cf884110a-1600x900.png)

__</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Choose a product (e.g. reusable water bottle).
* Identify the material, likely scale of production, and propose a suitable manufacturing technique.
* Justify your choice using at least **three factors** from the table above.</callout>
